channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Reseña de ‘La odisea’: un clásico en todo sentido
item.description: La película de Christopher Nolan logra algo inusual: conciliar el cine de autor con el cine comercial, envuelto en el encanto del viejo Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: La guerra de EE. UU. contra Irán entra en una nueva fase
item.description: Estados Unidos parece estar iniciando la segunda etapa de su campaña militar con un nuevo enfoque, aunque no necesariamente con una estrategia más clara.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Eric Schmitt and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump en la Oficina Oval el martes. La más reciente fase de la campaña militar de Estados Unidos en Irán tiene un nuevo enfoque, pero no necesariamente una estrategia más clara.

item.title: Argentina Advances to World Cup Final, Stunning England Fans
item.description: Argentina beat England 2-1 on Wednesday to reach their second successive World Cup final, leaving one side elated and the other heartbroken.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Sebastián López Brach for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Toro rojo o toro azul? En esta ciudad de Brasil, mantenerse neutral no es opción
item.description: En lo profundo de la selva amazónica, una intensa rivalidad divide a una ciudad desde hace un siglo, y no tiene nada que ver con deportes ni con política.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and Dado Galdieri

media:content.media:description: La arena de Parintins, Brasil, con los toros rojo y azul en la fachada, antes de una serie de presentaciones que determinarán cuál será el toro ganador del año.

item.title: La guerra en Irán implicará precios más altos para las empresas
item.description: El mundo en el que las empresas están operando se ha vuelto más peligroso e impredecible. Para adaptarse, hará falta flexibilidad. Y esa flexibilidad tiene su costo.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Linh Pham para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Las interrupciones en la cadena de suministro han elevado los costos y afectado la manufactura en todo el sudeste asiático.

item.title: Members of Congress Intensify Criticism of Immigration Detention Center
item.description: Lawmakers are continuing to urge action at Delaney Hall in Newark, where the conditions have been decried by detainees and demonstrators.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley

media:content.media:description: Demonstrations outside Delaney Hall in Newark have gone on for weeks.

item.title: How Roger Bennett, of Men in Blazers, Became America’s No. 1 Soccer Fan
item.description: A football fanatic, Roger Bennett left England and became a convert to the U.S. game. His wildly successful podcasting empire is spreading the gospel.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein

media:content.media:description: Roger Bennett, at the Manhattan offices of the Men in Blazers Media Network.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 16, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Pokes Fun at Joe Biden’s New Memoir
item.description: “It’s the second book Biden has written,” Fallon said of the former president. “The first was ‘The Odyssey.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Hong Kong Police Raid Independent Bookstores and Arrest 5 People
item.description: Other booksellers have been detained in recent months as part of a broad national security crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson

media:content.media:description: Police officers loading confiscated items from Have A Nice Stay, an independent bookstore in Hong Kong on Wednesday.

item.title: The Race for A.I. Data Centers Arrives in India
item.description: India is developing new data centers to secure a foothold in the global A.I. race. Our South Asia bureau chief, Mujib Mashal, discusses how a new Google campus’s potential use of the region’s already strained energy and water resources is creating anxiety among local residents.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal, Sutton Raphael and June Kim

item.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
item.description: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Hari Kumar

media:content.media:credit: Priyadarshini Ravichandran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The port city of Visakhapatnam in the state of Andhra Pradesh is rapidly transforming into India’s coastal gateway for global tech infrastructure, fueled by massive A.I. data centers.

item.title: Los análisis de sangre para detectar alzhéimer resultan prometedores
item.description: Nuevos estudios arrojan luz sobre cómo las pruebas podrían transformar el tratamiento de la enfermedad y la predicción del riesgo a desarrollarla en personas sanas.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith and Pam Belluck
item.category: Journal of the American Medical Assn

media:content.media:credit: Claire Merchlinsky/The New York Times; fotografías vía Getty

item.title: An American Strike Killed Iranian Civilians. Where’s the Accountability?
item.description: The Trump administration has failed to publicly address the mistaken missile strike that killed children during the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: W.J. Hennigan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Quote of the Day: Ukraine’s Next Phase: The Battle for Minds
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, July 16, 2026.

item.title: Europe Wants to Break Free From American and Chinese Technology. But How?
item.description: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Mark Landler

media:content.media:description: Sébastien Lecornu, the French prime minister, has called for France to increase its independence from American technology firms, announcing plans to replace Zoom with a French-developed version.

item.title: In Iran war, Trump Risks Another American ‘Forever War’
item.description: President Trump, who promised to “end wars,” not start them, may have fallen into a familiar presidential trap.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A judiciary building damaged in the war as seen from the Golestan palace, in central Tehran, Iran, this month.

item.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs on Brazil
item.description: The United States accused the country of unfair trade practices. The tariff will replace ones that the Supreme Court struck down.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ana Ionova

media:content.media:description: President Trump first imposed tariffs on Brazil last year in an effort to help former President Jair Bolsonaro avoid a jail sentence.

item.title: New York City Considering Banning Horse Carriages
item.description: The City Council considered on Wednesday whether to ban horse-drawn carriages, following recent tragic incidents, including the death of a tourist in June.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling, Jorge Mitssunaga and Jackeline Luna

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 16, 2026
item.description: You’ll get a kick out of Zhou Zhang and Mallory Montgomery’s puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Michael Hanson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “Sir! Sir! You look incredible — who are you wearing this evening? Is the collar Dior?”

item.title: Iranian American Woman Held in Iran on Spying Charges Is Released
item.description: The dual Iranian-U.S. citizen, Dena Karari, had her passport seized in December 2024 and had not been able to leave Iran as she faced espionage charges.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Los casos de ciclosporosis van en aumento. Esto es lo que debes saber
item.description: El parásito ha enfermado a miles de personas en todo Estados Unidos. Los expertos tienen consejos sobre cómo protegerte del brote.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi

media:content.media:credit: Claire Merchlinsky/The New York Times; Fotografías vía Getty

item.title: How Penn Station Could Run More Trains
item.description: The Trump administration plans an $8 billion overhaul. A U.S. study recommends extending platforms and adding staircases or escalators to increase the number of trains at peak times.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration plans to upgrade Pennsylvania Station in Manhattan.

item.title: Fate of Blanche’s Nomination Could Rest on One Republican Senator
item.description: Senator John Cornyn, Republican of Texas, may have the deciding vote on Todd Blanche’s confirmation as attorney general. Even a single Republican “no” vote would block Mr. Blanche’s nomination, and the senator said after Wednesday’s meeting that he had not made up his mind.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche on Wednesday at his confirmation hearing to be attorney general. It is not clear when the Senate Judiciary Committee will schedule a vote.

item.title: Trump Says He’ll Seek to Replace Immigrant Truck Drivers With Veterans
item.description: The president has pushed to curtail the commercial licenses of immigrants, including legal residents, whom he has blamed for crashes.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:description: President Trump mentioned a plan to crack down on truck drivers in the United States without legal status and to replace them with veterans during a panel at a military investment summit in Carlisle, Pa., on Wednesday.

item.title: Investigators Seek Information From Government Officials as Part of Air Force One Leak Investigation
item.description: After The New York Times reported on security concerns related to the Qatari-donated jet, the F.B.I. sought to speak with several people who flew aboard the plane with President Trump last week and asked for their phones.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Glenn Thrush
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump on July 1 at Joint Base Andrews next to the jet donated by Qatar, before its first flight as Air Force One.

item.title: New York Times Files Motion to Quash Subpoenas of Its Journalists
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel grand jury testimony as part of an investigation into Times reporting on the president’s new Air Force One jet.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:description: President Trump boarding the new Air Force One at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland this month.

item.title: NextEra and Dominion File to Form a Huge Power Company
item.description: They asked regulators in three states and at a federal agency to approve a deal that would create a combined company with 10 million customers across the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn

media:content.media:description: NextEra Energy’s headquarters in Juno Beach, Fla. Florida does not require regulatory approval of NextEra’s deal with Dominion Energy, but the companies filed requests with Virginia, North Carolina and South Carolina.

item.title: Suspect in Utah Stabbing Wanted to Kill Muslims, Court Filing Says
item.description: A Muslim man who works at a mall near Salt Lake City is hospitalized with multiple wounds. Charges have not yet been filed against the suspect, who was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto

media:content.media:description: Syed Sohail Uddin was taken to the hospital and is being closely monitored.

item.title: For England, the World Cup Final Remains Elusive
item.description: The nation’s years of pain continue.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:description: England’s loss prolongs a World Cup drought that has been a national pain point.

item.title: FBI Evidence Team Visits Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: Officials surveyed the newly drained pool, where the floor began peeling soon after a $16 million repair job. President Trump has blamed vandals.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett and David A. Fahrenthold

media:content.media:credit: Demetrius Freeman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Investigators examining the drained Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Boat That Sank Near Alcatraz Rolled After Wave Struck, Officials Say
item.description: Rescuers described survivors trying to stay afloat in the San Francisco Bay. A brother of the boat’s owner died, and the authorities say they will end the search for the missing.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla, Jacey Fortin and Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:description: A San Francisco Fire Department boat searching near Alcatraz Island for three missing people after a recreational boat, the Volare, sank on Tuesday.

item.title: Video Pushed Local Police to Come Clean. D.H.S. Is Ignoring the Lessons.
item.description: The agency has said little about two fatal encounters in the past week. Local police departments have learned to be forthcoming.
item.dc:creator: Shaila Dewan and Esmé E. Deprez

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo near the site where he was killed in Houston.

item.title: Door-to-Door Search for Grapevines Sold at Costco Begins in California’s Santa Clara County
item.description: Officials in Santa Clara County began knocking on doors on Monday to collect potentially infested plants sold at some Costco stores.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri

media:content.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Santa Clara County also warned that desert willows sold at certain Costco locations across 15 Northern California counties between late June 24 and July 3 might also be affected.

item.title: Trump Administration Fires U.S. Attorney Minutes After His Appointment
item.description: Federal judges had chosen the veteran Seattle prosecutor to fill the vacant seat. Now, a potential legal battle looms.
item.dc:creator: Danny Hakim and Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:description: Roger Rogoff, a former federal prosecutor and state judge, in court in Seattle in 2016.

item.title: House Republican in Key Swing Race Discloses a Drunk-Driving Arrest
item.description: Representative Mike Lawler of New York said in a televised interview that he was arrested on St. Patrick’s Day in 2012, after learning that his father was gravely ill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:description: Representative Mike Lawler, Republican of New York, is facing a stiff re-election challenge as his party seeks to maintain control of Congress.

item.title: G.O.P. Appears to Boost Socialist’s Primary Campaign for Wisconsin Governor
item.description: Republicans seem to view Francesca Hong as their weakest opponent in a general election. They are spending $2.2 million in an apparent attempt to aid her primary campaign.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Some Democrats have fretted that state Representative Francesca Hong, a democratic socialist with unabashedly left-wing views, might win her primary and then deliver the governor’s mansion to Republicans.

item.title: Parents of Teen Killed During Horse Carriage Ride Plead for a Ban
item.description: The death of Romanch Mahajan appeared to have swayed a majority of New York City Council members to vote to abolish the 167-year-old horse-carriage industry in two years.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan and Miles G. Cohen
item.category: New Yorkers for Clean, Livable and Safe Streets

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Romanch Mahajan, 18, died when a horse bolted after a carriage driver stepped out to take a photo.

item.title: Maine Democrats’ Adventurous Political Experiment
item.description: The party’s leaders have a political experiment on their hands.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood

item.title: A Key Senator Says He Is Undecided on Trump’s Attorney General Pick
item.description: Also, the World Cup final is set. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche on Capitol Hill today.

item.title: Iran’s Top Negotiator Signals Door to Diplomacy Is Still Open
item.description: Facing intense divisions within Iran, Gen. Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf both struck a defiant tone and argued that diplomacy could defend Iran’s interests.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, center, at the funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran this month.

item.title: Hegseth Plans to Screen All Troops, Including Women, for Low Testosterone
item.description: Pete Hegseth, as defense secretary, has sought to cultivate an image as a manosphere-friendly leader.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Azeen Ghorayshi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: The goal, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a social media message, was a “High-T Department of War.”

item.title: Democrats Question Trump’s Top Intelligence Pick Over 2020 Elections
item.description: Jay Clayton, President Trump’s nominee to lead the nation’s intelligence community, was repeatedly questioned by Democrat senators about the 2020 election during his confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:description: The Senate Intelligence Committee is expected to vote on Jay Clayton’s nomination early next week.

item.title: PEN America Defends Israel Article That Led to President’s Resignation
item.description: A co-chief executive of the free expression group said a recent article about Israeli and Jewish writers had been “critical” to pursue.
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Ord/Getty Images For Pen America
media:content.media:description: Clarisse Rosaz Shariyf, left, and Summer Lopez were named co-chief executives of PEN America in February.

item.title: Here Are the Maine Democrats Running to Replace Graham Platner
item.description: To win, they’ll have to convince Democrats at a July 25 convention that they’re the best candidate to take on Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

item.title: Ukraine’s Minister of Defense, a Proponent of Drone Warfare, Steps Down
item.description: Mykhailo Fedorov announced he was leaving the ministry on Wednesday after conflicts with generals and military contractors over the role of innovative weaponry.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mykhailo Fedorov in Kyiv last March.

item.title: England Lost in the World Cup. They Were Always Going to Lose.
item.description: The football team, like Britain itself, is burdened by the dead weight of impossible expectations.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Rose

item.title: Guy Scott, Who Caused a Stir as White Leader of Zambia, Dies at 82
item.description: Two decades after the fall of apartheid in South Africa, he served briefly as Africa’s only white president.
item.dc:creator: Alan Cowell

media:content.media:description: Guy Scott in 2013, while serving as the vice president of Zambia. Earlier, as agriculture minister, he was credited with rescuing his country from potential famine after a severe drought.

item.title: Is Grunge ... Back?
item.description: The grunge track “Freakin’ Out” by Dexter and the Moonrocks has been quietly climbing the charts and racking up millions of streams. Our music critic Jon Caramanica breaks down how the song, with its raw vocals and instrumentation, calls back to ’90s and 2000s radio rock.

item.title: Will Iran Be a Forever War?
item.description: President Trump may have fallen into the same trap that his predecessors did in Iraq and Afghanistan. Our chief diplomatic correspondent explains why.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
